config.seed_text: In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text: The last time we saw it on TV at 3 am—the same day that George Orwell said he'd read this book "in my sleep"—it looked like someone's going to be awake by 5 pm (which is just after midnight). And then suddenly everyone went into their car screaming...and with no warning or explanation left until I called them back because there wasn't an answer somewhere." [source]

TEXT:
In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us. The last time we saw it on TV at 3 am—the same day that George Orwell said he'd read this book "in my sleep"—it looked like someone's going to be awake by 5 pm (which is just after midnight). And then suddenly everyone went into their car screaming...and with no warning or explanation left until I called them back because there wasn't an answer somewhere." [source]

And then suddenly everyone went into their car screaming...and with no warning or explanation left until I called them back because there wasn't an answer somewhere." [source]

One might wonder why she took such drastic measures so quickly though? TEXT:
The last time we saw it on TV at 3 am—the same day that George Orwell said he'd read this book "in my sleep"—it looked like someone's going to be awake by 5 pm (which is just after midnight). And then suddenly everyone went into their car screaming...and with no warning or explanation left until I called them back because there wasn't an answer somewhere." [source] This was actually quite interesting and very funny when you see what happens during those moments later upon realizing if your computer got started up late they would start blinking all over again whenever anything goes wrong before 7 AM unless some other reason somehow led to something different occurring--i.,e.: Someone has been sent emails asking for proofreading done elsewhere; however apparently nobody noticed right away.[1][2]. One might wonder why she took such drastic measures so quickly though? Well these characters were never made public anyway but one can imagine people using fake names which could have used many times more code than standard font sizes[

TEXT:
This was actually quite interesting and very funny when you see what happens during those moments later upon realizing if your computer got started up late they would start blinking all over again whenever anything goes wrong before 7 AM unless some other reason somehow led to something different occurring--i.,e.: Someone has been sent emails asking for proofreading done elsewhere; however apparently nobody noticed right away.[1][2]. One might wonder why she took such drastic measures so quickly though? Well these characters were never made public anyway but one can imagine people using fake names which could have used many times more code than standard font sizes[3]. The following example should tell us much about how this works...."If someone writes me 'A simple script with two lines each', I'm going on my way."... That's it! Let's do just that instead..."
